hero.title: The walkthrough.
hero.lead: This is a guided tour of how Neo works — no code, no setup. We follow a single request all the way through the loop, show who does what at each step, and end with a few commands you can run yourself.

mindset.title: Hold onto this.
body: You say what you want. Neo turns it into tracked work, drafts it with AI, hands it to a real person to check, and brings it back to you for the final yes.
That's the whole thing. Everything below is just that one sentence, slowed down so you can see each step happen.
mindset.pull: AI drafts. A person verifies. You decide. Nothing goes out without all three.

roles.title: Three players.
roles.intro: Only three people (well — two people and Neo) touch any request. Knowing who does what makes the rest obvious.

items.name: You
items.note: You speak the request and you give the final yes. That's it — you never touch the tools in between.

items.name: Neo
items.note: Sorts the request, drafts it with Claude, opens the branch and pull request, and tracks the status. All the plumbing.

items.name: The reviewer
items.note: A contracted person who checks the draft, asks for changes if needed, and signs off — seeing only the work, never the jargon.

walkthrough.title: Follow one request.
walkthrough.intro: Say you want a proposal for the Red Cross. Here's every step it takes, who does it, and what you'd actually see on screen.

steps.name: Speak the request
steps.happens: You say what you want in plain language — “draft a sponsorship proposal for the Red Cross.” Neo captures it as a tracked ticket.
steps.see: A new card appears on the board, in the To-do column.

steps.name: Neo sorts it
steps.happens: Neo tags the request: which area of life it belongs to, how urgent it is, how long it'll take. Hot items start right away; the rest wait in a queue.
steps.see: The card moves into In progress, with its area and priority filled in.

steps.name: Claude drafts it
steps.happens: Neo picks the right skill (“nonprofit proposal”), asks Claude to write a first draft, saves it to a shared file, and opens a pull request — all automatically.
steps.see: The card moves to In Review. A complete first draft now exists.

steps.name: An expert checks it
steps.happens: The reviewer opens the draft in the web app, reads it, and either signs off or asks for changes. If they request changes, it loops back to step 3 for another pass.
steps.see: A clean draft with two buttons — Approve and Request Changes. No ticket numbers, no code.

steps.name: You approve
steps.happens: Once the expert signs off, it comes to you. Approve it and it's done. Send it back and it returns to the expert for another round.
steps.see: The card moves to Done, parked for your final sign-off.

walkthrough.loopNote: Reject at any point and the work loops back to “in progress” — the same way a control system re-runs its loop until the target is met.

reviewer.title: Three clicks, that's it.
reviewer.intro: The reviewer's whole job is three screens. This is what they'd do for the Red Cross draft.

steps.name: Open the queue
steps.desc: They land on the review queue and see every draft waiting for them — each with a clean, human title.

steps.name: Read the draft
steps.desc: They click one and read the full proposal. No Jira key, no branch name, no jargon — just the work itself.

steps.name: Decide
steps.desc: They click Approve, or write a note and click Request Changes. Neo handles everything that happens next.

reviewer.loopNote: Request Changes sends the note to Claude and re-opens the draft — the reviewer never has to explain it twice.

tryit.title: Run it in three steps.
tryit.intro: Want to poke at it live? Here's the whole thing, in order — no accounts or keys required. It all runs in demo mode with sample proposals; the first step needs only a terminal, the rest open in a browser.

items.name: Watch the loop run
items.desc: Narrate the whole loop on the Red Cross example — all five steps print out, no keys needed.

items.name: Open the app
items.desc: Start the web app with one command — it frees the port and launches in demo mode — then open the address it prints (http://127.0.0.1:8000).

items.name: Walk the screens
items.desc: Speak a request at /new, watch it land, then play the reviewer at /reviews — open the Red Cross draft and click Approve.

items.label: You only speak and approve
items.detail: The two ends of the loop are yours. Neo and the expert do everything in between.

items.label: Nothing goes out without a human
items.detail: AI drafts, but a real person checks every piece, and you give the final yes.

items.label: No jargon ever leaks
items.detail: The reviewer and you see clean work — never a ticket number, branch, or line of code.

items.label: Reject just loops it back
items.detail: Sending something back isn't a dead end — it re-runs the draft step automatically.

footer.line: Neo — now you've seen the loop
footer.credit: Walkthrough by Mariah · Arlington, VA · June 15, 2026
